incorrects.description: Student selected 2 × 8: did not recognize that rotation changes the row expression

params.description: Array highlights to show current orientation: 2 rows of 8.

steps.text: That's the expression now, before rotating. When we rotate, rows become columns and columns become rows. What will the new row count be?

incorrects.description: Student selected 16 × 2: used the total instead of the row structure

params.description: Array highlights to emphasize row structure.

steps.text: 16 is the total dots, not the number of rows. After rotation, the 8 columns will become 8 rows. How many dots will be in each row?

incorrects.description: Student selected 8 + 2: used addition instead of multiplication

params.description: Array highlights rows to show multiplication structure.

steps.text: Arrays use multiplication, not addition. We count rows times dots per row. After rotation, we'll have 8 rows. How many dots will each row have?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Array now shows 8 rows of 2 dots each.

steps.text: Let me show you how rotation works. The 2 rows become 2 columns. The 8 columns become 8 rows. Now we have 8 rows of 2 dots. The row expression is 8 times 2. Rotation switches the numbers in the multiplication.
